stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • A villanelle is a nineteen-line poetic form.
  • It uses two repeating rhyme sounds and two repeating refrain lines according to a set pattern.
  • “Late Hours” is a villanelle by Naomi Whitfield.
  • Its refrain lines are “The lamp burns slowly down” and “Sleep follows what was found.”
  • “Tide-Bound” is a villanelle by Naomi Whitfield.
  • Its refrain lines are “The shore forgets the sea” and “The day returns to me.”
stem: The student wants to use one of the poems to illustrate the villanelle form. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: As a villanelle, “Tide-Bound” contains nineteen lines and uses two repeating refrain lines—in this case, “The shore forgets the sea” and “The day returns to me”—according to a set pattern.
Whitfield employs the villanelle, a nineteen-line poetic form with two repeating refrain lines, in both “Late Hours” and “Tide-Bound.”
The nineteen-line villanelle form uses the refrain lines “The shore forgets the sea” and “The day returns to me,” which appear in the poem “Late Hours.”
Whitfield has used the villanelle form multiple times, as in “Tide-Bound,” which contains these refrain lines: “The lamp burns slowly down” and “Sleep follows what was found.”

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer because it uses one poem (“Tide-Bound”) and its actual refrain lines to illustrate the structural features of the villanelle.

Choice B references both poems but doesn't illustrate the form. Choice C misassigns the refrains. Choice D also misassigns refrains.
